themes: AI job displacement & profession boundary competition

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The clearest articulation yet of the 3-layer ambient-intent interface stack (capture → orchestration → execution), directly relevant to where AI product UX is heading.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Levie names two underappreciated reasons agents cause overwork: leverage on incremental effort is suddenly huge, and expectation recalibration hasn't caught up — useful framing for AI product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Gell-Mann amnesia applied to AI job displacement — people underestimate last-mile complexity in others' jobs while experiencing it firsthand in their own, a key nuance for sales enablement narratives.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Parker Harris publicly questioning why anyone should ever log into Salesforce again is a remarkable signal of how SaaS incumbents are pricing in their own disruption — directly relevant to sales enablement.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison pushes back on bad agent security conclusions and gives the two correct rules — important operational guardrail for anyone building or selling production agents.

worthClicking.author: @minchoi
worthClicking.why: Anthropic confirmed a billing bug in Claude Code (HERMES.md triggering API billing instead of Max plan) then refused a refund — concrete trust and pricing-risk story for enterprise buyers.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick introduces sociological profession-boundary theory to the AI jobs debate, predicting regulatory battles over who gets to use AI in law and medicine — a more durable framing than the usual economist take.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Comprehensive curated Claude Code resource bible thread — useful reference for indie builders evaluating the tooling ecosystem.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Frames Anthropic as running a deflationary bundle (five products for $20) against Adobe's $60+ — a useful competitive framing for AI product strategy and positioning conversations.

voices.why: Two sharp posts this cycle on agent overwork paradox and AI job displacement nuance — consistently useful for AI product strategy thinking.
Building an agent-native home screen product and writing the most concrete HCI reinvention content in the feed — directly on the OS/interface reinvention thesis.
Technically grounded, quick to correct bad conclusions — reliable signal on agent architecture and security.
Bringing underused sociological frameworks to the AI displacement debate; less hype, more durable analysis.

examples: RoundtableSpace crypto/meme engagement bait (GM posts, Bitcoin polls, 'what are we aping')
aakashgupta off-topic lifestyle content (marathon results, LeBron, bird sounds, USSR photos)
DataChaz low-engagement thread fragments with single-digit views

emerging.theme: AI job displacement & profession boundary competition

fading.theme: AI adoption phase shift: revenue over research
